channel.title: Comments for page "Snakes And Ladders"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Snakes And Ladders"

item.title: Re: Love the character development!
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for the nice comment! I'm glad you enjoyed the tale and the characterization of the protagonist. It really makes me happy to hear that people like reading slice-of-life stories about everyday people at the Foundation. :)

item.title: Love the character development!
item.content:encoded:
I love tales like this, those that introduce a character with all their strengths and flaws, and actually tell a story based around that character. Especially liked how it wasn’t about how a so-and-so Keter broke containment and terrorized Site what’s-its-name, but rather about how everyday people in the Foundation go about their lives. This story just placed Zyn as one of my favorite characters in the Foundation, alongside Marion Wheeler from the antimemetics division.

Phenomenal job, an absolute +1 from me.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: I appreciate your doing the thing and I'm glad you loved the tale!

I remember reading this, and loving it.
But I didn't +1'ed it, so I re-read it and did the thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fucking ace.

YEET.

Why did I take so long to start on Resurrection?

It kind of ran out of steam and hasn't been updated in awhile, so totally understandable!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking ace. Why did I take so long to start on Resurrection?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much for the nice comment! :) I'm glad to know that the protagonist in the tale is relatable; I try really hard to write developed characters people can identify with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite possibly some of the best character writing on the site. Zyn was immediately relatable and human, and that's not even getting into the colorful prose.

I couldn't upvote harder if I tried.

item.title: Re: This is good
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed the read! Thanks for the comment. :)

item.title: This is good
item.content:encoded:
Well, I have to say, this is an excellent story.

Now I think I grok a bit more about SCP-2332, knowing where it came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
mlister linked me to someone who did a reading of this tale! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C75wH3sEcpA

Big thanks to An Extremely Agitated Hedgehog on YouTube for this recording!

ETA: Oh man, just finished listening to the whole thing. I love the variation in voice pitch and tone for the different characters! The ending was amazing. Gave me shivers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale and the accompanying SCP are what got me to finally join the site.

Oh my goodness! This means a lot to me, and I'm so glad that you enjoyed the tale!

Thanks for the comment, and welcome to the site. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been reading SCPs off and on for years (like, before Series II, I think). This tale and the accompanying SCP are what got me to finally join the site. Very, very well done.

I love that Zyn redefines her priorities instead of getting hung up forever on 408.

εїз εїз εїз εїз εїз εїз εїз

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, thanks! :3c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
;w; Thanks~
But for real, lovely work. Bit by bit made me care for the character, root for their goals, and cheer them along the way…
By the way, I forgot to upvote! So I'll send this little fella to take care of it~ …~εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: /passes over a tissue box anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't make me tear up.
At all
I'M NOT CRYING. YOU ARE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had a science teacher named Mercer for years, and they are weirdly similar.

Aside from this, this was an excellently written tale. Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, thank you. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, thank you very much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This line confuses me, though.

Zyn told her interns about her idea (the usage of "pitch" isn't in relation to sound tone but in that "pitching an idea" is putting it out there for feedback/approval) and they were so excited and noisy that they had to leave the cafeteria because they were disturbing the other people eating there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this one. For me personally, it was like a pep talk in tale form. The pacing and the hooks were nicely done especially.

I don't think I can find anything in particular to critique, so I'll stick with compliments. Solid work, Zyn &co. You done did good.

EDIT:

Her pitch was met with enough enthusiasm to get her interns kicked out of the cafeteria.

This line confuses me, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you plan to include 2332 or Zyn in actual A-9 tales?

Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm…

Again: discussion about spoilers
Ok, this callapsing is getting tedious...

Well, i actually meant a species of butterflies, rather than this single one.
So let's call that a hit.
Unfortunately i didn't know that (IC)Zyn has a brother…
Damn, i didn't know that he has a whole hub behind him!
Thanks for info :)
New hub to explore, Woo-hoo!


Oh, and one question: Do you plan to include 2332 or Zyn in actual A-9 tales?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An older version of the draft had a mention of Zyn's older brother, but that tidbit was trimmed for length. Mark Kiryu isn't mentioned at all here because he does a lot of traveling, plus he hops between Site-19 and Site-17 and just leaves Riven to take care of Kiryu Labs when he's out working with an SCP or something. (edit: if it's not clear, Riven's promotion in this story was when he first joined Kiryu Labs)

IC-Zyn tends to feel a little smothered by her big bro at times. But she'll still wear the matching turtleneck sweater.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually meant to ask, is there a Mark in this universe? It doesn't seem like there is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoileerrrr disssccussioonn
hide me

Close! Zyn in this universe created a butterfly that was the prototype for the butterfly that later on got stuck in another universe where it was given number 2332.

The interview in SCP-2332 is actually held by Mark Kiryu, Zyn's older brother and director of Kiryu Labs.

As of this story, the butterfly essentially has all the abilities of 2332 (made of light, can speak, can think) except the parallel universe hopping.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. Guess i'm overinterpretating again :\
(but you gotta admit, it kinda seems like that, doesn't it?)
Also:

Another spoilerous(?) theory
Hit?

This whole story actually takes place in:

the Foundation in some other time/space/whatever [where they] managed to manufacture a means to send messages between parallel dimensions.

So Zyn in this universe (this story) created a butterfly that later on got stuck in other universe where it was given number 2332 and was interviewed by other Zyn.


Anyway, hope to see either 2332 or (IC)Zyn in actual A-9 stories. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…huh. I actually didn't see it that way.

I dunno if these are spoilers, but to be on the safe side...
yeah okay they're probably definitely spoilers

There are at least a few traits that in-character Zyn Kiryu and IRL Zyn share, but I didn't write the tale to mirror the SCP writing process. I do write a lot of "day in the life"/slice of life stories for the wiki though, so this is sort of an extension of that, plus setting up a character for Resurrection.

Also, Zyn Kiryu doesn't actually create SCP-2332, but rather, a preliminary prototype of it. What she actually does with the light butterfly will be revealed later. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So if I get this correctly:

Spoiler(?)
Or, did i get it wrong way?

This whole story is an allegory of writing a SCP on wiki:
First you become member, but that's nothing outstanding. Then you get you first idea which get denied/downvoted. Then you start thinking. Looking for friends on site. Gaining experience. Learning tricks. Studying how to write. Soon, you become reckognizable among users. And then, you start writing your skip. Asking people for help/ideas/proofreading/etc.
And finally, you have your first succesfull SCP.


Please correct me if i'm wrong.
Anyway, great tale. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. I've lately heard writing advice that says your character needs to want something in the first scene. This is a perfect example of that advice in action. Zyn doggedly pursues her goal, understanding the importance of pursuing smaller goals along the way, until such time when she finds something different but equally important to do. Very compelling stuff, and a good treatise on how to develop professional relationships.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started off by not being good enough to work with the butterflies and ended up working directly under Dr. Mann and creating her own SCP. Impressive career arc.

item.title: Re: Freezer Fairy :>
item.content:encoded: I had the same problem XD except I didn't work at the library, apparently there were rumors that I was an apparition….. My school mates IQs were not high XD

item.title: Re: Freezer Fairy :>
item.content:encoded: I always wondered about people getting squished in movable book racks like that…

item.title: Re: Freezer Fairy :>
item.content:encoded:
wat

(Incidentally, the "fairy" nickname comes from when I worked in my high school library. There's a stack of moving book racks in the back vault where all the class books were kept. For some reason no one could hear me moving around when I went to shelve textbooks, so they'd just move the racks and almost squish me between partitions.)

item.title: Freezer Fairy :>
item.content:encoded: I like how thoughtful it is and how it all works together. Your nickname is now officially Freezer Fairy. This can not be rescinded, you are, and for ever shall be Freezer Fairy. :>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very long piece, but interesting enough to have my attention the whole way through. +1

item.title: Re: A few quick remarks
item.content:encoded: Whooops. Missing word added, thanks. xD;;;

item.title: A few quick remarks
item.content:encoded:
I looked up to when I first met her.

I looked up to her when I first met her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It wasn't anger, she told herself. She was just making the trash more space-efficient.

Oh, a like-minded soul. How unexpected to find you here, dear.

UPVOTED.

item.title: Author Notes
item.content:encoded:
Okay, wow. This took a long, long time to write. I think it was conceptualized a few months ago, only started getting written up two months ago, and has gone through 9001.5 revisions (interspersed by me playing Pokemon games until inspiration whacked me over the head again) up until now.

Many, many thanks to these awesome people:

  • Clef for an insane amount of trimming help and brainstorming scenes and dialogue and also direct edits seriously Clef thank you so much
  • thedeadlymoose for helping with plotline, characterization, and technique (scorpion scene!)
  • DrMann for character cameo and phrasing help he is good with the words yes
  • Riemann for being tough on the pacing and plot development
  • Decibelles for scrutinizing the word choice
  • Eskobar for pointing out inconsistencies in character portrayal
  • Dexanote for discussing overall feel of the piece
  • Kain for commenting on inherent characterization and buildup
  • MacLeod, Gaffsey, username_null, tahunu, FoggyGolem, lurkd, NRuien, Petrograd, and MisterFlames for reading it in chat
  • And of course, if I forgot anyone, my apologies. You’re all the best.
